latin: Newton non solum de malis cogitabat. eadem vis quae malum traxit etiam lunam tenet. luna perpetuo cadit circa terram sed numquam eam attingit. haec idea simplex universum explicavit.

questions.prompt: What does Newton realize about the moon?
questions.target: It is perpetually falling around the Earth
choices: It is perpetually falling around the Earth
It was once part of Earth

questions.prompt: What connects the apple and the moon?
questions.target: The same force acts on both
choices: The same force acts on both
They are the same size relatively
Both were created at the same time

translation: Newton was not only thinking about apples. The same force that pulled the apple also holds the moon. The moon perpetually falls around the Earth but never reaches it. This simple idea explained the universe.
